jfx_data_status.description: JFX0729 corrected data has been fully processed on BOTH local and server. Local: 2026-06-16 (scripts/prepare_jfx_fixed_data.py, 7/7 checks). Server: 2026-06-16 (same script, 7/7 checks, DESKTOP-HKP0IV0). All old JFX data archived on both sides.

server.existing_zscored: HYZ15040 + LMZ12939 already z-scored in ssGSEA_zscore/, no re-processing needed

jfx_data_status.next_action: defer JFX token-cache rebuild; a new data transformation is planned and caches/retraining should be rebuilt only after that transform is finalized and applied consistently
jfx_data_status.retraining_naming_convention: All JFX-corrected retraining runs MUST use --dataset_name ending in _jfxfix20260616

cache_status.HYZ15040: local exists (uni2h_cache_tokens, 2655 .pt); server status not reverified in this update
cache_status.LMZ12939: local exists (uni2h_cache_tokens, 7513 .pt); server status not reverified in this update
cache_status.JFX0729: missing locally — rebuild deferred pending the upcoming unified data transformation

jfx_data_status.reference_doc: 01_指南与解读/分析报告/JFX0729数据替换后重跑实验清单.md

nine_patient_status.description: 9-patient full cohort data was expected around early July 2026, but this checkout has not confirmed its arrival as of 2026-06-30. Current work remains on the 3-patient core matrix; JFX token-cache rebuild and P0 retraining are deferred pending an upcoming unified data transformation.

experiments.notes: Formal server run; local copy lacks training_history.csv. Serves as legacy_firstN Transformer baseline.
GFNet legacy_firstN formal result. Serves as historical reference for frequency-branch experiments. NB: likely included register tokens in the 65-token sequence.

experiments.decision_gate: A0b serves as the clean-token (cls_patch64) baseline for A1 comparison.
experiments.notes: GFNet cls_patch64 (CLS + 64 patch tokens, no register). Excludes register tokens from FFT sequence. Train-Val Gap narrows slightly vs legacy (0.160 vs 0.168).

experiments.decision_gate: A1 vs A0b: modReLU degraded val_loss (-2.2%), val_pcc (-6.1%), Train-Val Gap widened (+50%). Gate: FAIL → frequency sparsification hypothesis disproven. A2 (frequency augmentation) and A3 (AFNO) cancelled.
experiments.notes: modReLU magnitude threshold truncates weak frequency components, discarding subtle tissue texture info. Some pathways improved (MYC, Wound Healing) but more degraded (Coagulation, Inflammatory Response, E2F, OxPhos). Epoch window delayed (E4 vs E2) but overall worse.

experiments.decision_gate: B1a vs B1b: peak PCC 0.3956 vs 0.3955 — nearly identical. Frequency branch adds no incremental value over mean pooling. Both below CLS Frozen baseline (0.4113). Gate: FAIL → frequency side-branch disproven for CLS mode.
experiments.notes: CLS + GFNet frequency branch on patch tokens. Confounding variable: token_select_mode changed from legacy_firstN to cls_patch64 simultaneously with freq_branch addition. CLS cls_patch64 alone may already degrade vs legacy. Without freq=none + cls_patch64 control, cannot fully isolate freq_branch effect.

experiments.decision_gate: B1b (mean pool) same as B1a (gfnet) → frequency branch has no mechanism advantage. Both < CLS Frozen 0.4113. The CLS+patching side-branch architecture itself may not help.
experiments.notes: Non-frequency control for B1a. Mean pooling on patch tokens as side branch. Result confirms frequency processing is NOT the differentiator — both branches perform similarly below baseline.

experiments.decision_gate: C1 Val PCC ≥ 0.41 → Gate: PASS. Best Token-mode result ever (0.4169, +6.5% vs GFNet frozen legacy). Surpasses CLS Frozen (0.4113) but still below CLS LoRA (0.4322). Token+LoRA is viable; need Fold2/3 cross-validation.
experiments.notes: LoRA r=8 on UNI2-H backbone + GFNet TokenEncoder + cls_patch64. First time Token mode breaks 0.41. LoRA gain (+5.1% vs frozen) consistent with CLS LoRA gain. Most pathways show positive gain; Wound Healing, Coagulation, Angiogenesis, ECM show largest improvements. Train-Val Gap wider (0.194 vs 0.160 frozen) but manageable.

experiments.decision_gate: S1a >= A0b+0.01 (>=0.4067) -> cls_pool8x8 replaces cls_patch64 as Token route default. < A0b+0.01 -> spatial coverage not bottleneck; proceed to Step 2.
experiments.notes: Zero-parameter spatial sampling fix. CLS + 2x2 avgpool over full 16x16 grid -> 8x8=64 tokens. Unlike cls_patch64 (top 25% only), covers 100% spatial area with avg denoising. Sequence length unchanged (65), GFNet filter params identical.
experiments.jfx_note: Fold1 — JFX0729 in training set (JFX+LMZ→HYZ). Results are historical reference only; must re-run with corrected JFX data.

experiments.decision_gate: S1b >= C1+0.01 (>=0.4269) -> spatial coverage is key bottleneck for Token+LoRA route. < C1+0.01 -> spatial coverage alone insufficient, proceed to Step 2 post-hoc smoothing.
experiments.notes: Token+LoRA r=8 + cls_pool8x8. JFX FIXED: corrected data synced 2026-06-16. When launching, MUST use --dataset_name ending in _jfxfix20260616. Old planned run_dir has no results (was never started), can safely reuse or rename.

MPP-3 train (6 patients, ~42K patches) -> MPP-2/XZY external test. JFX named 'JFX' in MPP root. NaN guard: nan_to_num on raw; clip +-100 only AFTER z-score transform. Codex review fixes applied 2026-07-02: ddof=1, coord matching, metrics key fix, pred order, --no-val guard, HF_HOME, allow_missing.
